mnemonics: FINITE is KEY: A Family of closed sets with the finite Intersection property has Non-empty Total Intersection if the space is compacT.
keyPoints: A metric space is compact if and only if every family of closed sets with the finite intersection property has a non-empty intersection.
The finite intersection property (FIP) means that every finite subcollection of the family has a non-empty intersection.
This theorem provides a powerful dual characterization of compactness in terms of closed sets, complementing the open cover definition.
commonMistakes: Assuming the converse is always true without the closed set condition (the sets in the family must be closed).
Forgetting that the FIP must hold for every finite subcollection, not just some finite subcollections.
Confusing this with the open cover definition and trying to apply finite intersection ideas to open sets directly.
